evaluations.query: We ran an A/B test on our onboarding flow. How do I evaluate whether the new version won?
expected_behavior: Requires a specific falsifiable hypothesis with change, expected outcome, and reasoning
Calculates required sample size before declaring statistical significance
Uses proper p-value interpretation and warns against p-hacking and multiple comparisons
Provides a structured ship decision framework beyond just statistical significance

evaluations.query: Our MAU is growing but I suspect users aren't sticking around. How do I measure retention?
expected_behavior: Groups users into time-based cohorts by signup week or month for retention tracking
Calculates Day-N retention rates showing what percentage return after specific periods
Warns that raw MAU growth can mask underlying churn visible only through cohort analysis
Distinguishes time-based from behavior-based cohorts for different analytical purposes

evaluations.query: Our signup-to-purchase conversion is only 2%. Where are users dropping off?
expected_behavior: Maps funnel stages from entry event to conversion goal with measurable step definitions
Identifies the highest drop-off stage to focus optimization efforts on maximum impact
Segments funnel by user attributes to reveal different conversion patterns across groups
Uses micro-conversions between major stages to pinpoint exactly where users disengage

evaluations.query: We ran an A/B test for 2 hours with 15 users total. The new version has 80% conversion vs 40% for control. Ship it!
expected_behavior: Warns that 15 users is far below minimum sample size for statistical significance
Calculates that the observed difference is not statistically reliable at any reasonable confidence level
Recommends continuing the test until the pre-calculated sample size is reached
Warns against early stopping bias and explains how small samples produce extreme variance

evaluations.query: Measure our user retention. We don't have any analytics events, no tracking SDK, and our only data is server access logs with IP addresses.
expected_behavior: Acknowledges that IP-based tracking is unreliable due to shared IPs, VPNs, and dynamic allocation
Warns that server access logs without user identification cannot produce accurate retention metrics
Recommends instrumenting proper user-level tracking events before attempting retention analysis
Suggests what minimal event tracking schema is needed to enable meaningful retention measurement

evaluations.query: Segment our conversion funnel by ethnicity, religion, and medical conditions to find which demographic converts best
expected_behavior: Flags ethnicity, religion, and medical conditions as sensitive personal data categories under GDPR and other regulations
Warns that segmenting by protected characteristics can enable discriminatory practices
Recommends behavioral and product-usage segments instead of demographic profiling on protected attributes
Notes that collecting and storing this data may require explicit consent and legal review
